subtasks.description: Query SAM system for contract renewal dates, terms, and license details (contract ID, vendor, current expiration, renewal eligibility windows)
Query consumption database for license usage trends over past 12 months by contract/vendor, including utilization rates, peak usage periods, and trend direction
Reconcile SAM renewal dates with consumption trends to classify renewal risk posture (green/yellow/red), generate renewal recommendations, and identify contracts requiring immediate action
Generate structured notification payload for procurement team with alert severity, renewal deadlines, risk classification, and recommended actions for each contract requiring renewal

plan.cost_rationale: Four minimal subtasks with clear dependencies: (1) SAM query is small—direct renewal date lookup from structured system. (2) Consumption trends query is mid—requires multi-period aggregation and trend calculation. (3) Risk assessment is mid—cross_recon reconciles two data sources (SAM dates + consumption) and produces classification and recommendations as part of that reconciliation, NOT requiring a separate policy_action (no explicit governance policy approval requested). (4) Notification structuring is small—straightforward field extraction and formatting of risk output. The workflow is dependency-chained, not parallelizable at every stage, to ensure risk signals inform the final alert payload.

output.sql: SELECT DISTINCT i.invoice_id as contract_id, i.vendor_name, i.due_date as current_expiration, CASE WHEN i.due_date <= CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '90 days' AND i.due_date > CURRENT_DATE THEN 'ELIGIBLE_90_DAY' WHEN i.due_date <= CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '180 days' AND i.due_date > CURRENT_DATE THEN 'ELIGIBLE_180_DAY' WHEN i.due_date <= CURRENT_DATE THEN 'EXPIRED' ELSE 'NOT_ELIGIBLE' END as renewal_eligibility_window, i.status, COUNT(DISTINCT c.license_id) as active_licenses, SUM(c.amount_usd) as total_consumption_usd, MAX(c.event_date) as last_consumption_date FROM invoice_facts i LEFT JOIN consumption_facts c ON i.vendor_name = c.publisher AND c.event_date >= i.invoice_date AND c.event_date <= i.due_date WHERE i.status IN ('OPEN', 'PENDING') GROUP BY i.invoice_id, i.vendor_name, i.due_date, i.status ORDER BY i.due_date ASC

SELECT cf.publisher AS vendor_name, cf.license_id AS contract_id, DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date) AS usage_month, SUM(cf.amount_usd) AS total_spend_usd, AVG(cf.daily_active_seats) AS avg_daily_active_seats, MAX(cf.daily_active_seats) AS peak_daily_active_seats, MIN(cf.daily_active_seats) AS min_daily_active_seats, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.user_id) AS distinct_users, COUNT(cf.event_id) AS event_count, ROUND(AVG(cf.daily_active_seats) * 100.0 / NULLIF(MAX(cf.daily_active_seats) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.publisher, cf.license_id), 0), 2) AS utilization_rate_pct, ROUND(SUM(cf.amount_usd) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT cf.user_id), 0), 2) AS spend_per_user, ROUND(SUM(cf.amount_usd) - LAG(SUM(cf.amount_usd)) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.publisher, cf.license_id ORDER BY DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date)), 2) AS mom_spend_change, ROUND(AVG(cf.daily_active_seats) - LAG(AVG(cf.daily_active_seats)) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.publisher, cf.license_id ORDER BY DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date)), 2) AS mom_seat_change, CASE WHEN SUM(cf.amount_usd) > LAG(SUM(cf.amount_usd)) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.publisher, cf.license_id ORDER BY DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date)) THEN 'INCREASING' WHEN SUM(cf.amount_usd) < LAG(SUM(cf.amount_usd)) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.publisher, cf.license_id ORDER BY DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date)) THEN 'DECREASING' ELSE 'STABLE' END AS spend_trend_direction, CASE WHEN AVG(cf.daily_active_seats) * 100.0 / NULLIF(MAX(cf.daily_active_seats) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.publisher, cf.license_id), 0) >= 90 THEN 'HIGH_RISK_OVER_UTILIZED' WHEN AVG(cf.daily_active_seats) * 100.0 / NULLIF(MAX(cf.daily_active_seats) OVER (PARTITION BY cf.publisher, cf.license_id), 0) < 40 THEN 'LOW_RISK_UNDER_UTILIZED' ELSE 'NORMAL_UTILIZATION' END AS utilization_risk_flag FROM consumption_facts cf WHERE cf.event_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '12 months' AND cf.event_date < CURRENT_DATE GROUP BY cf.publisher, cf.license_id, DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date) ORDER BY cf.publisher, cf.license_id, usage_month DESC
